message.content: The formula is Newton's Second Law of Motion, which is force equals mass times acceleration ($F = ma$). Because the gravitational force pulling on an object is directly proportional to its mass, the ratio of force to mass always results in the exact same acceleration. You must now use this specific equation to mathematically justify why doubling the mass does not change the acceleration of the ball.
